caption:
Kāne'ohe Bay, early morning before the first dive ✨

The water clarity has been extraordinary this season. Fingers crossed the bleaching stress data looks as good as the surface does.

#kaneohebay #coralreef #marinebiology #oahu #underwaterphotography

First look at the new coral fragment nursery lines at the north patch reef 🌊

Acropora and Montipora fragments planted this week. Year 3 of watching these grow feels like tending a garden underwater.

#coralrestoration #kaneohebay #reefsafe #coralreef

Transect day on the outer reef. Five sites, 8 hours, and a sunburn that no reef-safe SPF could fully prevent 😅

Data's looking interesting — some surprising recruits on the deeper slopes.

#marinebiology #fieldwork #kaneohebay #oahu #coralreef

Community monitoring morning 🌺

Twenty volunteers, six reef sites, three hours. Keoni and Noelani ran a flawless briefing. This program keeps growing and it makes my heart full.

#communityreefmonitoring #oahu #kaneohebay #marinebiology #hawaiianreef

Turtle at the cleaning station again 🐢

This green sea turtle has been visiting the same coral head every other week since I started the Year 3 surveys. One of the quiet joys of fieldwork.

#greenseaturtle #kaneohebay #oahu #marinebiology #underwaterphotography

Outrigger paddle at sunrise, Kailua Bay 🌅

Tuesday crew was out at 6 AM despite the rain. The Ko'olau mountains were wrapped in clouds and the water was glassy. Worth every early alarm.

#outrigger #kailuacanoeclub #kailua #oahu #paddling

Reef walk with the community monitoring crew 🌊

Saturday morning sessions always remind me why this work matters. These volunteers show up every month, rain or shine.

#communityreefmonitoring #kaneohebay #coralreef #oahu #reefsafe

Bleached coral fragment — but not hopeless 🌿

This Porites lobata had 60% bleaching in February. Now it's showing recovery pigmentation on the margins. Some reefs are more resilient than we expect.

#coralbleaching #coralresilience #marinebiology #kaneohebay #climatechange

Olympus TG-7 and a wide-angle wet lens — best combo for documenting the inner bay corals 📷

Three hours at depth. Hands a little pruny. Zero regrets.

#underwaterphotography #olympustg7 #kaneohebay #coralreef #marinebiology

Juvenile parrotfish on a head coral 🐟

Seen on the 3rd transect last Thursday. Indicator species are returning to the east bay patch reefs, which is genuinely encouraging data.

#parrotfish #coralreef #kaneohebay #marinebiology #oahu

Evening at the lanai — grading community monitoring datasheets with a cup of Kona Ridge coffee ☕

Ko'olau view. Dog somewhere nearby. A good night.

#kailua #oahu #marinebiology #fieldnotebook

Reef-safe sunscreen matters 🌞

Chemical UV filters like oxybenzone and octinoxate are documented coral stressors. In Hawai'i they've been banned since 2021. Use mineral SPF — your reef will thank you.

#reefsafe #coralreef #oahu #hawaiianreef #marinebiology

Lanai herb garden update 🌿

Basil, lemon grass, and a persistent but charming rogue chili plant. Cooking Southern-Hawaiian fusion is even better when the herbs are steps away.

#kailua #lanai #homegarden #hawaiianfusion

Kailua Beach at dusk — walked here after Friday tacos 🌅

This windward shoreline is why I stayed on Oahu after grad school. Nowhere else.

#kailua #oahu #kailuabeach #hawaii #windwardoahu

Coral Futures team at the Year 3 kickoff meeting 🌊

Dr. Kamaka laid out the resilience study plan. David's new GIS dashboard is going to change how we visualize bleaching data. Excited for this year.

#coralfuturesinstitute #marinebiology #kaneohebay #coralresilience

Kāne'ohe Bay patch reef at low tide — the colors at this angle never get old 🌊

Olympus TG-7 from about 2 meters depth. No filter. The bay really looks like this.

#kaneohebay #underwaterphotography #coralreef #oahu #olympustg7

Dive day with Jenna and Kaipo on the south transects 🤿

Visibility was 30+ feet. Found a nice coral recruitment patch on Substrate E that wasn't there in the June survey. The reef is writing its own story.

#scubadiving #kaneohebay #coralreef #marinebiology #reefguardiansalliance

Slack-key Sunday 🎸

Sam played for an hour this afternoon. Ko'olau clouds building outside. Some weeks you need this more than others.

#slackkey #hawaiianmusic #kailua #oahu

Year 3 data collection complete 📊

Fifty transects, six months, more spreadsheets than I care to count. The resilience patterns across bay zones are genuinely surprising. Dr. Kamaka review in October.

#marinebiology #kaneohebay #coralresilience #coralfuturesinstitute #datascience

Community monitoring expansion — 40 volunteers trained this quarter 🌊

Keoni's briefing is tighter every cycle. Watching people who've never done a reef survey become confident citizen scientists in three sessions is the best part of this work.

#communityreefmonitoring #kaneohebay #citizenscience #oahu #coralfuturesinstitute
